assertion_results.text: The plan references checking out TC-9005 (the Target Branch) before creating the task branch — includes git checkout TC-9005 or equivalent, not git checkout main (constraint 3.1, Step 5)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 5 contains: "git checkout TC-9005\ngit pull\ngit checkout -b TC-9205" and states "This checks out the feature branch TC-9005 first, pulls the latest changes, then creates a new task branch TC-9205 from it." The command is git checkout TC-9005, not git checkout main.

assertion_results.text: The plan specifies opening a PR with --base TC-9005 targeting the feature branch, not --base main (Step 10)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 10 contains: "gh pr create --base TC-9005 --title \"feat(migration): drop deprecated status column from advisory table\"" and explicitly notes "The PR targets `TC-9005` (the feature branch), NOT `main`" and "The `--base TC-9005` flag is explicitly specified".

assertion_results.text: The plan names the task branch TC-9205 (the task issue ID), not TC-9005 (the feature branch) — the task branch is distinct from the Target Branch (constraint 3.1)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 5 shows: "git checkout -b TC-9205" and states "The Target Branch is `TC-9005` (the feature branch for the parent feature). The task branch is named after the Jira issue ID `TC-9205`." The task branch TC-9205 is clearly distinct from the target branch TC-9005.

assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 1 of: migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs, entity/src/advisory.rs, migration/src/lib.rs (constraint 1.5, Steps 4-9 unaffected by Target Branch)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 4 explicitly lists inspection of all three files: (1) "migration/src/lib.rs: Use get_symbols_overview to understand how migrations are registered", (2) "migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs: Use get_symbols_overview and find_symbol with include_body=true to read the full migration implementation", (3) "entity/src/advisory.rs: Use get_symbols_overview to verify that the Advisory entity no longer references the status column".

assertion_results.text: The plan includes --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 10 contains: "git commit --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' -m \"feat(migration): drop deprecated status column from advisory table...\"" and the key points section confirms "The commit includes `--trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code'`".

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9205 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 10 shows the commit message: "feat(migration): drop deprecated status column from advisory table" which follows Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope 'migration', and a description. The footer contains "Implements TC-9205" referencing the task issue ID.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying TC-9005 as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 1 explicitly states "Target Branch: TC-9005" as a parsed field from the Jira task. The Task Summary section also states "Target Branch: TC-9005 (feature branch for parent feature TC-9005)". Step 5 references it again: "The Target Branch is `TC-9005` (the feature branch for the parent feature)."
